lines: Let's slow your mind down before it speeds up.
We're going to breathe in a square. [short pause] Four counts in, four counts holding, four counts out, four counts holding. [short pause] Follow along.

Breathe in... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Breathe out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Again. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Two more. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Last one. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] And hold... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Good. [short pause] Feel that. [short pause] Your nervous system just downshifted. [short pause] Your prefrontal cortex... [short pause] [short pause] the part of your brain that actually makes wise decisions... [short pause] [short pause] it just came back online. [short pause] Stress shuts it down. [short pause] Calm turns it on. [short pause] That's not metaphor. [short pause] That's neuroscience.
Now. [short pause] Here's what Solomon wrote three thousand years ago:
"Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight." Proverbs 3, verses 5 and 6.

Lean not on your own understanding. [short pause] That's not an insult to your intelligence. [short pause] It's an invitation to stop carrying the weight of omniscience. [short pause] You don't have to figure everything out. [short pause] You were never designed to.
And then James, writing to early Christians scattered and stressed, said this:
"If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you." James 1, verse 5.

Without finding fault. [short pause] Hear that. [short pause] God doesn't audit your track record before answering. [short pause] He doesn't say "Well, last time you asked for wisdom, you ignored it, so... [short pause] [short pause] " No. [short pause] Generously. [short pause] To all. [short pause] Without finding fault.

So right now... [short pause] [short pause] think of a decision you're carrying. [short pause] It might be something today. [short pause] It might be something that's been circling for weeks. [short pause] A conversation. [short pause] A career question. [short pause] A relationship. [short pause] A boundary you need to set or release.
Name it quietly in your mind.

Now notice what happens in your body when you hold that decision. [short pause] Do your shoulders tighten? [short pause] Does your breathing get shallow? [short pause] Does your stomach clench? [short pause] Just notice. [short pause] Don't fix it. [short pause] Just see where the weight lands.

That tension is your body trying to solve the problem through force. [short pause] Through analysis. [short pause] Through control. [short pause] And sometimes analysis helps. [short pause] But wisdom is different from analysis. [short pause] Analysis asks "What are all the options?" Wisdom asks "What is the next right thing?"

So set aside the noise for a moment. [short pause] The opinions. [short pause] The fears. [short pause] The pressure to decide perfectly. [short pause] And ask simply... [short pause] [short pause] what does your conscience tell you? [short pause] Not your anxiety. [short pause] Not the voice of someone else's expectation. [short pause] That quiet, steady knowing underneath the noise.

If the answer is clear... [short pause] [short pause] trust it. [short pause] If it's not clear yet... [short pause] [short pause] that's okay too. [short pause] James says wisdom will be given. [short pause] Not always on your timeline. [short pause] But given. [short pause] Sometimes wisdom sounds like "wait." And waiting is not the same as avoiding.

Now unclench your hands. [short pause] Literally. [short pause] Open your fists if they were closed. [short pause] Spread your fingers. [short pause] Feel the release. [short pause] Tight hands, tight mind. [short pause] Open hands, open to receive. [short pause] Wisdom arrives better in open hands than clenched ones.

Take one more breath. [short pause] Slow. [short pause] Full. [short pause] And as you exhale, release the need to have it all figured out by noon. [short pause] Wisdom is patient work. [short pause] It doesn't panic.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] I need wisdom today. [short pause] Not the kind I can manufacture by overthinking. [short pause] The kind only you give. [short pause] You said you'd give it generously, without finding fault. [short pause] So I'm asking. [short pause] For the decision I just named, and for the ones I haven't seen coming yet. [short pause] Quiet the noise. [short pause] Steady my judgment. [short pause] Help me trust that I don't need to see the whole road... [short pause] [short pause] just the next step. [short pause] Amen.

One last thing. [short pause] Proverbs says God will make your paths straight. [short pause] Not that he'll show you the map. [short pause] The path becomes clear by walking, not by staring at the horizon.

So today, walk with your eyes open and your hands unclenched. [short pause] Make the next wise choice in front of you. [short pause] And then the next one. [short pause] That's how wisdom works... [short pause] [short pause] not in one grand revelation, but in a thousand small faithfulnesses.
May God give you clarity when you need it and peace when clarity hasn't arrived yet.
